questionHtml:
Both vinegar (a dilute aqueous solution of ethanoic acid) and bleach are used as cleaning agents.

Bleach reacts with ammonia, also used as a cleaning agent, to produce the poisonous compound chloramine, NH2Cl.

Deduce the molecular geometry of chloramine and estimate its H–N–H bond angle.

 

Molecular geometry:

H–N–H bond angle:
markschemeHtml:
Molecular geometry:
«trigonal» pyramidal  [✔]

H–N–H bond angle:
107°  [✔]

 

Note: Accept angles in the range of 100–109.

meta.title: Deduce the molecular geometry of chloramine and estimate its H–N–H bond angle. Molecular geometry: H–N–H bond angle:
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.2.4—The valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) model enables the shapes of molecules to be predicted from the repulsion of electron domains around a central atom. Predict the electron domain geometry and the molecular geometry for species with up to four electron domains.
